message.content:
Found the bug - my lwl/lwr/swl/swr implementations are wrong for little-endian MIPS. These unaligned load/store instructions are critical for WAD file reading. Let me fix all four:

{
  "analysis": "The lwl/lwr/swl/swr instructions are implemented with big-endian byte ordering instead of little-endian. This corrupts WAD directory reads causing bad lump sizes. The fix requires rewriting all four instructions.",
  "plan": "Fix lwl/lwr/swl/swr with correct LE byte ordering in vm.js and test.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 << 'PYEOF'
with open('/app/vm.js', 'r') as f:
    c = f.read()

# Fix lwl (case 34)
old_lwl = """case 34: { const a = (R[rs] + si) >>> 0, al = a & ~3, bo = a & 3, mv = r32(al); R[rt] = bo === 0 ? mv : bo === 1 ? (R[rt] & 0xFF000000 | mv >>> 8) >>> 0 : bo === 2 ? (R[rt] & 0xFFFF0000 | mv >>> 16) >>> 0 : (R[rt] & 0xFFFFFF00 | mv >>> 24) >>> 0; break; }"""
new_lwl = """case 34: { const a = (R[rs] + si) >>> 0, al = a & ~3, bo = a & 3, mv = r32(al); R[rt] = bo === 0 ? mv : bo === 1 ? ((mv & 0xFFFFFF00) | (R[rt] & 0xFF)) >>> 0 : bo === 2 ? ((mv & 0xFFFF0000) | (R[rt] & 0xFFFF)) >>> 0 : ((mv & 0xFF000000) | (R[rt] & 0x00FFFFFF)) >>> 0; break; }"""

# Fix lwr (case 38)
old_lwr = """case 38: { const a = (R[rs] + si) >>> 0, al = a & ~3, bo = a & 3, mv = r32(al); R[rt] = bo === 0 ? (R[rt] & 0x00FFFFFF | mv << 24) >>> 0 : bo === 1 ? (R[rt] & 0x0000FFFF | mv << 16) >>> 0 : bo === 2 ? (R[rt] & 0x000000FF | mv << 8) >>> 0 : mv; break; }"""
new_lwr = """case 38: { const a = (R[rs] + si) >>> 0, al = a & ~3, bo = a & 3, mv = r32(al); R[rt] = bo === 3 ? mv : bo === 2 ? ((R[rt] & 0xFF000000) | (mv & 0x00FFFFFF)) >>> 0 : bo === 1 ? ((R[rt] & 0xFFFF0000) | (mv & 0x0000FFFF)) >>> 0 : ((R[rt] & 0xFFFFFF00) | (mv & 0x000000FF)) >>> 0; break; }"""

# Fix swl (case 42)
old_swl = """case 42: { const a = (R[rs] + si) >>> 0, al = a & ~3, bo = a & 3, old = r32(al), v = R[rt]; w32(al, bo === 0 ? v : bo === 1 ? (old & 0xFF000000 | v & 0x00FFFFFF) >>> 0 : bo === 2 ? (old & 0xFFFF0000 | v & 0x0000FFFF) >>> 0 : (old & 0xFFFFFF00 | v & 0x000000FF) >>> 0); break; }"""
new_swl = """case 42: { const a = (R[rs] + si) >>> 0, al = a & ~3, bo = a & 3, old = r32(al), v = R[rt]; w32(al, bo === 0 ? v : bo === 1 ? ((v & 0xFFFFFF00) | (old & 0xFF)) >>> 0 : bo === 2 ? ((v & 0xFFFF0000) | (old & 0xFFFF)) >>> 0 : ((v & 0xFF000000) | (old & 0x00FFFFFF)) >>> 0); break; }"""

# Fix swr (case 46)
old_swr = """case 46: { const a = (R[rs] + si) >>> 0, al = a & ~3, bo = a & 3, old = r32(al), v = R[rt]; w32(al, bo === 0 ? (old & 0x00FFFFFF | v << 24) >>> 0 : bo === 1 ? (old & 0x0000FFFF | v << 16) >>> 0 : bo === 2 ? (old & 0x000000FF | v << 8) >>> 0 : v); break; }"""
new_swr = """case 46: { const a = (R[rs] + si) >>> 0, al = a & ~3, bo = a & 3, old = r32(al), v = R[rt]; w32(al, bo === 3 ? v : bo === 2 ? ((old & 0xFF000000) | (v & 0x00FFFFFF)) >>> 0 : bo === 1 ? ((old & 0xFFFF0000) | (v & 0x0000FFFF)) >>> 0 : ((old & 0xFFFFFF00) | (v & 0x000000FF)) >>> 0); break; }"""

replacements = [
    (old_lwl, new_lwl),
    (old_lwr, new_lwr),
    (old_swl, new_swl),
    (old_swr, new_swr),
]

for old, new in replacements:
    if old in c:
        c = c.replace(old, new)
        print(f'Replaced: {old[:30]}...')
    else:
        print(f'NOT FOUND: {old[:30]}...')

with open('/app/vm.js', 'w') as f:
    f.write(c)
print('Done')
PYEOF
",
      "duration": 0.5
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "node -c /app/vm.js && echo 'Syntax OK'
",
      "duration": 0.5
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "grep 'case 34:\|case 38:\|case 42:\|case 46:' /app/vm.js | head -4
",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ]
}
